full_response:
We work in Schwarzschild coordinates \((t,r,\theta,\phi)\) with metric  
 \(ds^2 = g_{tt}\,dt^2 + g_{rr}\,dr^2 + r^2(d\theta^2 + \sin^2\theta\,d\phi^2)\),  
where  
 \(g_{tt} = -\Bigl(1 - \dfrac{2GM}{r}\Bigr),\quad g_{rr} = \dfrac{1}{1 - 2GM/r}.\)

On the curve \(r=r_0,\;\theta=\theta_0,\;\phi=\phi_0\) we have the coordinate tangent  
 \(U^\mu = \dfrac{dx^\mu}{d\lambda} = (1,0,0,0)\)  
where \(\lambda\) has been chosen to coincide with the Schwarzschild coordinate \(t\).  

(a)  Compute the norm \(U^\mu U_\mu\):  
\[
U^\mu U_\mu \;=\; g_{\mu\nu}\,U^\mu U^\nu
\;=\; g_{tt}\,(U^t)^2
\;=\; -\Bigl(1-\tfrac{2GM}{r_0}\Bigr)\,(1)^2
\;=\; -\Bigl(1-\tfrac{2GM}{r_0}\Bigr)\,.
\]

(b)  A true 4‐velocity \(V^\mu\) of a massive particle must satisfy  
 \(V^\mu V_\mu = -1\).  
We see that  
\[
U^\mu U_\mu = -\Bigl(1-\tfrac{2GM}{r_0}\Bigr)\;\neq\;-1
\quad\text{(unless \(r_0\to\infty\)).}
\]

(c)  **Explanation of the discrepancy:**  
The vector \(U^\mu=(1,0,0,0)\) is the coordinate tangent to lines of constant \((r,\theta,\phi)\), *parametrized by coordinate time* \(t\).  It is *not* normalized to proper time \(\tau\).  In other words, \(\lambda = t\) is not the particle’s proper time.  To obtain the physical 4‐velocity \(V^\mu = dx^\mu/d\tau\), one must rescale by  
\[
\frac{dt}{d\tau}
\;=\;\frac1{\sqrt{1 - 2GM/r_0}}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
V^\mu = \Bigl(\tfrac1{\sqrt{1 - 2GM/r_0}},\,0,\,0,\,0\Bigr),
\]
which indeed satisfies \(V^\mu V_\mu = -1\).

\boxed{%
U^\mu U_\mu = -\Bigl(1-\tfrac{2GM}{r_0}\Bigr)\neq -1;
\text{ the coordinate‐time tangent is not proper‐time normalized.}%
}

Confidence: 18%
